title: Distictis mansoana
intro: Distictis mansoana, also known as the "Man's Flower" or "Man's Flower Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including treating fever, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Distictis mansoana include alkaloids, such as distictine and manchine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Distictis mansoana is often prepared as a tea or infusion to treat respiratory conditions, or as a tincture to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Distictis mansoana is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to South America. The plant belongs to the genus Distictis, which is a part of the Bignoniaceae family. However, some sources classify it within the family Apocynaceae. Further taxonomic research is needed to determine the most accurate classification.
